name: Microsoft Solitaire Collection
version_note: Windows 11 Microsoft Solitaire Collection (UWP Store app). Contains Klondike, Spider, FreeCell, Pyramid, TriPeaks.

launch.method: Use Win+Search: press_key(keys=['win']), type_text('solitaire'), press_key(keys=['return']). open_app often fails for UWP apps.
startup_issues: May show internet error popup on launch — dismiss with Escape or click Cancel.
May show sign-in prompt — press Escape or click 'Not Now' or 'Skip'.
May show ad/video — wait 5s then press Escape, or look for 'Skip' or 'X' button.
May resume previous game — ALWAYS press F2 to start a new game.
May open a browser tab with an ad — DON'T close the browser, use focus_app('Solitaire') to switch back.
Settings/options dialog may open — press Escape to close it.
The app title in the taskbar is 'Solitaire & Casual Games' not just 'Solitaire'.
launch.fullscreen: Press F11 IMMEDIATELY after the app opens. This prevents clicking on background apps and makes cards larger and easier to read.

klondike.rules: Standard Klondike solitaire. 7 tableau columns, 4 foundation piles (top right), stock pile (top left), waste pile.
klondike.goal: Move all cards to the 4 foundation piles, building up from Ace to King in each suit.
klondike.tableau_rules: Build down in alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Kings can go on empty columns.
klondike.foundation_rules: Build up by suit from Ace to King (Ace first, then 2, 3... King).
klondike.stock: Click the stock pile (top left) to draw cards. When stock is empty, click to recycle.
klondike.new_game: ALWAYS press F2 to start a fresh new game. Do NOT try to resume a previous game.
tips: Double-click a card to auto-move it to the foundation if possible — much faster than dragging.
Always move Aces and 2s to foundations immediately.
Prefer uncovering face-down cards over other moves.
Empty columns are valuable — only fill them with Kings.
Draw from stock when no obvious tableau moves exist.
Use 'H' key for hints if stuck — the game will highlight a valid move.
Press H multiple times to cycle through available hints.

ui_layout.note: Positions are APPROXIMATE for fullscreen at 2560x1600. The game centers content. ALWAYS use vision to verify actual positions.
ui_layout.stock_pile: Top-left of the game area. Usually around x=350-450, y=300-400 when fullscreen.
ui_layout.waste_pile: Right of stock pile. Usually around x=550-650, y=300-400.
ui_layout.foundations: Top-right area, 4 piles spaced horizontally. Usually x=1100-1900, y=300-400.
ui_layout.tableau: 7 columns below the top row, spread across the screen. Usually y=550+ when fullscreen.
ui_layout.menu_gear: Small gear icon in top-left or top-right corner — DO NOT click it, it opens settings.
ui_layout.game_selector: When first opened, shows game tiles (Klondike, Spider, etc). Click Klondike tile to start.

known_issues: open_app(app_exe='solitaire') often FAILS for this UWP app. Use Win+Search instead.
App may show ads between games — wait or press Escape.
Internet error popup appears if offline — press Escape to dismiss.
Sign-in prompts appear periodically — press Escape to skip.
The app sometimes opens a browser tab with an ad — NEVER close the browser, use focus_app to switch back.
Clicking the gear/settings icon opens a settings panel that blocks the game — press Escape to close.
Card positions change every game — ALWAYS use vision (look action) to locate cards before clicking.
The vision model may struggle to read small card values — use look with specific questions like 'What card is at the bottom of column 3?'
DO NOT declare DONE unless you have actually played multiple card moves. Opening the app is NOT completing the task.
Coordinates from previous games are WRONG for new games — always look first.
strategy_for_agent: 2. Focus: focus_app('Solitaire & Casual Games'). If that fails, try focus_app('Solitaire').
4. Dismiss popups: press_key(['escape']) twice to clear any dialogs/ads.
5. New game: press_key(['f2']) to start a fresh Klondike game. Wait 2 seconds.
6. Read the board: use look('Describe all 7 tableau columns. For each column, list the face-up cards from top to bottom with their value and suit.').
7. Identify moves: look for Aces (move to foundations via double-click), or cards that can stack (alternating colors, descending).
8. Make moves: double_click on a card to auto-move to foundation, or drag(x1,y1,x2,y2) between columns.
9. Draw from stock: click the stock pile (top-left) when no tableau moves available.
10. Use hints: press_key(['h']) if stuck — the game highlights a valid move.
11. NEVER spend more than 1 look action in a row. Look once, then ACT on what you see.
12. NEVER declare DONE until you have made at least 5 actual card moves (click, double_click, or drag on cards).
